mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes, designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models while optimizing performance through intelligent load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for specific resource configurations and potential costs associated with scaling.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://github.com/substratusai/kubeai/releases/download/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeAI repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/substratusai/kubeai/releases/download/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/kubeai-crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for KubeAI.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeAI operator in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage AI inference workloads.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the KubeAI pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

kubectl get crd

```

> This command lists all pods in the kubeai namespace.
> Verifies that the KubeAI CRDs are installed.

steps.title: Create an example KubeAI custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample custom resource for KubeAI to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.io/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  modelPath: /path/to/model
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Creates a sample model resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.io/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  modelPath: /path/to/model
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the custom resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the custom resource to ensure it is ready for use.

```bash

kubectl get model example-model -n kubeai

```

> Verifies the status of the created model.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all custom resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstalls the KubeAI Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your custom resources. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Backs up existing models.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KubeAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD YAML file may be missing or incorrectly formatted.

**Fix:**
Ensure the CRD file is accessible and correctly formatted, then reapply it.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application inside the pod may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Custom resource not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The custom resource may not have been created or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Recreate the custom resource using the provided YAML definition.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified version of the Helm chart may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the Helm chart version and update the repository with 'helm repo update'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.io/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  modelPath: /path/to/model
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.28 or higher with sufficient resources.
